purpose: Group confirmed roster units by motion biomechanics so post-pilot production can batch safely without copying one animation pose across unrelated characters.

sourceReferences.head: 5a3a8eec7991a98aad6f3acf0ce38687764dcb1a
sourceReferences.use: six-state controller, fallback semantics, marker/runtime conventions

sourceReferences.head: b20e4e349ca7627ca86f098c0be8d9edf9a54dde
sourceReferences.use: distinct Class 1 attack concepts; batching must not erase class-specific attack identity

sourceReferences.head: 292660b54ee01b5ab0df854c56ef849c15d730fc
sourceReferences.use: Class 1 locomotion/idle differentiation and marker timing patterns

sourceReferences.head: b3db907dfd33a181fae34859770c872319ab994e
sourceReferences.use: approved chibi style anchors, migration boundaries, technical reuse policy
active Map 1 enemy/miniboss/boss roster; obsolete Warden/Champion excluded
Class 1/Class 2 range, skill ownership and combat role evidence

principles: An archetype is a production/biomechanics grouping, not permission to clone poses or identity.
Units may have one primary archetype plus secondary tags.
Idle and Move can batch more broadly than Basic Attack because attacks depend on weapon/anatomy semantics.
Skill motion is never assumed required just because the runtime supports a skill state.
Boss/unique units default to individual review even when they borrow technical validators from an archetype.
Obsolete roster entries must not enter production waves unless separately reactivated by game-data scope.

archetypes.label: Humanoid Ranged
secondaryRules: same locomotion family does not imply same weapon attack
bow users may share draw/release review gates only when weapon biomechanics actually match

archetypes.label: Humanoid Melee
secondaryRules: do not batch a fist strike and sword slash as one pose template

archetypes.label: Small Creature / Deformable
secondaryRules: squash/stretch pipelines may become reusable only for similarly deformable bodies
do not apply slime deformation to skeletal or rigid monsters

archetypes.label: Quadruped / Beast
secondaryRules: Stone Wolf is the first confirmed Map 1 quadruped and should establish the locomotion archetype before future beasts fast-path

archetypes.label: Heavy Humanoid / Construct
secondaryRules: weight, anticipation and impact readability are primary
Golem also appears in normal encounters and Stage 5 miniboss pool, so locomotion is required

archetypes.label: Ranged Monster
secondaryRules: reuse ranged marker schema but preserve monster identity/material

archetypes.label: Assassin / Fast Unit
secondaryRules: fast locomotion and sharp anticipation can share review language
warp/backline logic remains runtime-owned and must not be baked into motion

archetypes.label: Boss / Unique
secondaryRules: unique scale/anatomy/multi-phase readability defaults to individual production
shared validators and review artifact tooling may still be reused

archetypes.label: Humanoid Specialist / Support
secondaryRules: biped locomotion can batch with humanoids
gadget/economy/support attack and skill gestures require profile-specific review

excludedFromCurrentWaves.reason: exists in GDD/legacy art but is not in current hero-balance production roster; require explicit current-runtime need before motion production

REQUIRED_NOW: idle for every active combat unit with approved production identity
move for every unit that changes grid position or approaches attack range
basicAttack for every unit that performs basic attacks
REQUIRED_LATER: skill when a distinct cast/telegraph/marker is needed beyond the existing attack fallback, especially bosses and signature mechanics
special summon/spawn motion when a gameplay feature actually exposes it
bespoke death for bosses/units whose death readability cannot rely on the existing death fade
OPTIONAL_POLISH: dedicated hit reaction while existing hit-flash remains sufficient
dedicated death animation for ordinary units while existing death-fade/fallback remains acceptable
minimumMotionPolicy.runtimeEvidence: PR #23 supports idle/move/attack/skill/hit/death but has deterministic fallbacks: move->idle, skill->attack->idle, hit->idle, death->hit->idle; hit reuses existing hit-flash and death reuses existing death-fade. Therefore six-state support is not a six-state production requirement.
